notes: Empty credentials (identifier='', secret='') correctly return 401 with 'Invalid credentials.' — only the empty-string guard is working.
The session cookie has no HttpOnly or Secure flags set: Set-Cookie: session=ok; Path=/
POST /logout also returns 404, suggesting logout is unimplemented on both GET and POST.

observations.value: POST /session with completely invalid/nonexistent credentials (e.g., identifier='wrong@example.com', secret='wrongpassword' or identifier='notauser@nowhere.com', secret='abc') returns HTTP 200 with Set-Cookie: session=ok; Path=/ and a 'Welcome' home page, instead of HTTP 401 with an 'Invalid credentials' error page. Authentication is not actually validating credentials — any non-empty identifier/secret pair is accepted and a session is granted.

POST /session with a valid-looking username but wrong password (identifier='alice@example.com', secret='wrongpassword') returns HTTP 200 with Set-Cookie: session=ok; Path=/ and a successful login page, confirming that password verification is completely bypassed.
GET /logout returns HTTP 404 (Not Found) instead of a valid response. The logout link shown after login points to /logout but that route does not exist.
